title: rubus fruticosus subsp. glandulosus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. glandulosus, also known as the Himalayan blackberry, is a species of blackberry native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. glandulosus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural remedy for wounds and skin issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
